summary: Moved nearly all remaining GALE01 split definitions out of `config/GALE01/splits.todo.txt` into the canonical `config/GALE01/splits.txt`, turning the todo file from a 4407-line split list into a tiny remainder with explicit `// WARNING: ninja hangs forever` notes. The PR also adjusted `configure.py` so the imported split map and object list remained buildable: it introduced a `fix_epilogue` switch that selects `GC/1.2.5n` versus `GC/1.2.5`, added extra include/system-include paths for Dolphin and runtime libs, marked several previously Matching objects as NonMatching, removed duplicate/alternate object entries, and enabled `fix_epilogue` for the pad and card libraries.

key_files.role: Canonical split map expanded by about 4040 added lines, importing most file/section address ranges from the todo list.
Large todo split list removed; only a small remainder remains, including warnings around splits that make ninja hang.
Build object metadata updated to accommodate the newly canonicalized splits and compiler-version/epilogue differences.

what_changed: `config/GALE01/splits.todo.txt` dropped from a full split inventory to a 10-line remainder, deleting 4399 lines and adding 2 warning/comment lines.

`config/GALE01/splits.txt` gained thousands of section ranges for Melee, Dolphin, MSL, and sysdolphin objects, including `.text`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, `.sdata2`, `extab`, and `extabindex` ranges.
A missing `melee/lb/lbcollision.c` `.sdata` range was added to `splits.txt`.

Formerly separate `melee/lb/lbrefract_data.c`, `lbrefract1.c`, `lbrefract2.c`, and `lbrefract3.c` todo split entries were represented in canonical `splits.txt` as combined `melee/lb/lbrefract.c` section ranges.
`configure.py` added a `fix_epilogue` parameter to `Lib`, using it to choose `mw_version` as `GC/1.2.5n` when enabled and `GC/1.2.5` when disabled.
`DolphinLib` now accepts `fix_epilogue=False` by default and passes extra include paths `src/melee` and system include path `src/sysdolphin`; `RuntimeLib` received the same include additions.
Several object entries were changed from Matching to NonMatching, including examples from fighter character files, MSL, Dolphin dsp/dvd/gx/os/ai/ar/card, and sysdolphin baselib.
Duplicate or alternate object entries were cleaned up, for example removing separate duplicate paths such as some short-name fighter files and data-only entries while keeping canonical source paths.
`DolphinLib` calls for the pad and card libraries explicitly set `fix_epilogue=True`.
smart_moves: Centralizing almost all split ranges in `splits.txt` makes the active build configuration the single source of truth instead of keeping most known ranges parked in a todo file.
Keeping the problem splits in `splits.todo.txt` with explicit `// WARNING: ninja hangs forever` markers preserves known hazards instead of silently importing entries that break the build workflow.
The `fix_epilogue` parameter is a compact build-system abstraction for libraries that need the `GC/1.2.5n` compiler variant, avoiding hardcoding one CodeWarrior version globally.

Marking affected objects NonMatching while importing their split ranges separates address-map progress from source-matching progress, allowing splits to advance without falsely claiming functions are matched.

Adding scoped include/system-include paths to Dolphin and runtime library definitions addresses cross-tree include needs at the library wrapper level rather than repeating them on individual object entries.
decomp_lessons: A large split-map import should be paired with configure/object-list corrections; merely moving ranges into `splits.txt` can expose objects that should not be treated as Matching yet.
When canonicalizing split data, preserve build-breaking ranges in a visible todo file with an explanation, especially if tooling can hang rather than fail cleanly.
Use compiler-version switches such as `fix_epilogue` when matching depends on CodeWarrior epilogue behavior; the same project may need both `GC/1.2.5` and `GC/1.2.5n` depending on the library.
Split entries should include all relevant sections for an object, not just `.text`; small data sections like `.sdata`, `.sbss`, and `.sdata2` can matter for accurate layout.
Canonical split imports are infrastructure work: they may lower apparent matching status for some objects but improve future decomp by making section boundaries explicit and searchable.
naming_conventions: GALE01 split entries use source-relative paths ending in `.c` followed by section rows with `start:0x... end:0x...` ranges.
Section names in split files are tab-indented and aligned, including `.text`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss`, `.sdata`, `.sbss`, `.sdata2`, `extab`, and `extabindex`.
Fighter common action files follow `melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_<Action>.c`, e.g. `ftCo_Attack1.c`, `ftCo_Damage.c`, and `ftCo_Guard.c`.

Character-specific fighter files use character abbreviations in canonical character directories, such as `ftKirby/ftKb_Init.c`, `ftNana/ftNn_Init.c`, `ftPurin/ftPr_Init.c`, and `ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c`.
Unknown or address-derived files retain names like `ft_07C1.c`, `ft_081B.c`, `ef_061D.c`, `pl_040D.c`, and `hsd_3B33.c`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use split imports to describe object/section layout even before the corresponding C sources are fully matching.
Downgrade objects to `NonMatching` when canonical split ranges expose that the source cannot currently be built as matching.
Use `GC/1.2.5n` selectively via `fix_epilogue=True` for libraries that need epilogue-fix behavior, while leaving other Dolphin libraries on `GC/1.2.5`.
Represent multi-section objects in one canonical split entry where appropriate, as seen with the imported `melee/lb/lbrefract.c` ranges.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Which split entries remained in `config/GALE01/splits.todo.txt` after PR1094 and why do they make ninja hang?
Which libraries still require `fix_epilogue=True` after the split import?
Which objects were changed from Matching to NonMatching in PR1094 and have they since been rematched?
Did later PRs remove the include-path HACK comments for `src/melee` and `src/sysdolphin` in `configure.py`?
